subject: Re: How can I visualize the picked particles?
body:
Hi Pranav,
In the star file generated using warp template matching function, the 
coordinates of the particles seem to be very small. Therefore, when I 
opened the star file in Cube, it seems that I picked particles totally 
wrong.  Did I do something wrong?
The template is low-pass filtered to 10A. The parameters used in template 
matching and the star file are attached.
[image: parameter.png]
在2022年11月11日星期五 UTC+8 18:21:47<aijia wen> 写道：

> Thank you very much for your help!
>
> 在2022年11月11日星期五 UTC+8 18:18:03<p.sha...@gmail.com> 写道：
>
>> Warp is not designed to display tomgrams, or tilt series. You can use 
>> Cube to visualise the coords (https://github.com/dtegunov/cube) or you 
>> can convert the coords in the star file to an imod model file. Depending on 
>> your programmatic skills you could either -
>> a) edit the star file manually using a simple text editor such as notepad 
>> and remove all columns except for the 3D coordinates and provide that txt 
>> file as an input to imod point2model program (
>> https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/man/point2model.html) which will 
>> generate a mod file for you. YOu can then use IMOD to visualise the 
>> tomogram with the mod file which will contain your hits.
>> b) You can use Alister Burt's imodmodel and starfile python libraries to 
>> achieve the same effect as in (a).
>>
>> Bonus -
>> If you use cube then you need to put a check mark against the option that 
>> states "coordinates are normalised".
>>
>> Best,
>> Pranav
>> --
>> Pranav Shah
>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>
>> Division of Structural Biology,
>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>> University of Oxford,
>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>> UK
>>
>>
>> On Fri, Nov 11, 2022 at 10:07 AM aijia wen <abett...@126.com> wrote:
>>
>>> After I reconstruct the full tomogram, the Fourier Space section looks 
>>> fine, but there's nothing in the Real Space section, I'm not sure if it is 
>>> normal. Did I do something wrong?[image: Fourier space.png][image: Real 
>>> space.png]
>>
>> 在2022年11月11日星期五 UTC+8 17:01:08<aijia wen> 写道：
>>>
>>>> Hi all,
>>>>
>>>> I used a template to pick 1200 particles in a tomogram, but the 
>>>> generated star file only presents the coordinates. Is there any way that I 
>>>> can visualize the picked particles in Warp?
>>>>
>>>> Thanks in advance for your help!
>>>>
>>>> --
